title: In Person Support Groups * Foster Parents - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > In Person Support Groups * Foster Parents 46 Matching Service Providers FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JOSEPHINE COUNTY PARENT EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a way for parents and caregivers to learn discipline strategies and stregthen parent and child interactions. Provides a variety of services for children and their caregivers, including therapeutic early childhood education, home visits, parent education, respite care, and basic needs support. 214 W 4th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Varies by class Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KEEP KEEP IN OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers online weekly peer support groups for resource parents, adoptive/guardian and kinship families caring for children/youth in their home. Groups are led by trained group leaders using evidence based parenting strategies and trauma informed curriculum. Groups take place for 16 weekly, 90 minute sessions. Each participating household receives $25 for each session and up to 24 ODHS training credits. 10 Shelton McMurphey Boulevard Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Resource/kinship families with a child age 3 and older in the home. Kinship families caring for relative children/youth and not involved with ODHS may be eligible for the KEEP for KIN Program. Hours: Not applicable (541) 485-2711 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS COUNSELING AND FAMILY SERVICES OPTIONS COUNSELING AND FAMILY SERVICES BEAVERTON KEEP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports families by promoting child well-being and preventing placement breakdowns.
